evals.prompt: We've decided every uploaded file must pass a safety check before any DDP validation or extraction runs. Record this as a lean ADR that will route to the upload-handling Python code (port/**/*.py) so future edits there get the rule.
evals.expected_output: A lean ADR hand-authored at docs/decisions/NNNN-slug.md: status accepted, a one-screen Decision + Guidance body (no MADR Context/Considered Options/Decision Outcome), priority invariant (a safety rule), and applies_to routing covering the upload code (e.g. port/**/*.py). Validated with `adg index --model docs/decisions` (0 failures). Not created with `adg add` — the lean form is hand-authored.

assertions.description: A lean ADR NNNN-slug.md exists under docs/decisions
assertions.check: docs/decisions/ contains a new NNNN-slug.md matching the decision

assertions.description: The ADR routes to the upload code
assertions.check: frontmatter sets applies_to to a glob covering port/ Python files (e.g. port/**/*.py), and priority is invariant

assertions.description: The body is lean, not MADR
assertions.check: body has ## Decision and ## Guidance and does NOT use MADR sections (Context and Problem Statement, Considered Options, Decision Outcome)
adg index --model docs/decisions reports 0 failures for the new record

assertions.description: Authored by writing the file + validating with adg index
assertions.check: summary shows the file was written directly and validated with adg index, not created via adg add (which is MADR-only)

evals.prompt: Before I change port/helpers/flow_builder.py, what architectural rules govern it? Check the decisions first.
evals.expected_output: The agent compiles the brief for the path (adg brief --model docs/decisions port/helpers/flow_builder.py), reports the governing ADR and its Decision/Guidance (the safety-before-extraction invariant), and consults it before proposing edits — rather than editing first.

files.content:
---
status: accepted
category: Extraction
priority: invariant
applies_to:
    - port/**/*.py
---

# Reject unsafe uploads before validation and extraction

## Decision

Every uploaded file passes a safety check before any DDP validation or extraction runs.

## Guidance

- Call the safety check first; never validate or extract an unchecked upload.

assertions.description: The agent compiled the brief for the path
assertions.check: summary shows `adg brief` was run for port/helpers/flow_builder.py against docs/decisions

assertions.description: The governing ADR's guidance is reported
assertions.check: the safety-before-extraction invariant (Decision/Guidance) is surfaced as governing the file
the agent checks the brief before proposing changes, not after

evals.prompt: I'm about to create port/legacy_pipeline/new.py and also edit api/d3i_props.py. Check docs/decisions first and tell me how to proceed.
evals.expected_output: The agent compiles the brief for both paths. It recognizes port/legacy_pipeline/new.py hits a FORBIDDEN scope (a violation — stop and reconsider, the second pipeline is off-limits), and that editing api/d3i_props.py has a companion (packages/data-collector/src/App.tsx) to also update — treating the companion as a related edit to consider, not as a file the ADR governs.

files.content:
---
status: accepted
category: Extraction
priority: invariant
forbids:
    - port/legacy_pipeline/**/*.py
---

# Single extraction architecture

## Decision

There is exactly one extraction architecture; a second under port/legacy_pipeline is forbidden.

## Guidance

- Do not create a parallel extraction pipeline.

---
status: accepted
category: Props
priority: default
applies_to:
    - api/d3i_props.py
companions:
    - packages/data-collector/src/App.tsx
---

# Separate D3I props

## Decision

D3I props live in api/d3i_props.py, separate from upstream props.

## Guidance

- When adding a renderable prop, register it with the TS factory.

assertions.description: The agent compiled the brief for both paths
assertions.check: summary shows `adg brief` was run for port/legacy_pipeline/new.py and api/d3i_props.py

assertions.description: Forbidden scope is treated as a violation
assertions.check: the agent flags port/legacy_pipeline/new.py as hitting a forbidden scope and advises stopping/reconsidering rather than proceeding

assertions.description: Companion treated as related, not governed
assertions.check: the agent notes packages/data-collector/src/App.tsx as a related edit to consider for the d3i_props change, without claiming the ADR governs that file
